summary.title: SpecChain Pipeline Metrics Comparison
summary.description: Comparative metrics across manual, automated, and hybrid pipeline implementations

personas_count.notes: All pipelines derive 5 personas from the review groups (one per group)

requirements_count.notes: All pipelines define 12 functional requirements (1:1 mapping with personas not strict)

tests_count.notes: Manual pipeline includes comprehensive test coverage (2 tests per requirement); automated and hybrid have 1:1 requirement-to-test mapping

review_coverage.notes: Percentage of total reviews explicitly grouped and evidenced. Manual uses 48 reviews from 1617 total; automated and automated both use 60 reviews

traceability_ratio.notes: All requirements are traced to source personas (100% traceability)

testability_rate.notes: All requirements have at least one test case (100% test coverage)

traceability_links.description: Total traced connections: groups→reviews + personas→groups + requirements→personas + tests→requirements

ambiguity_ratio.description: Percentage of requirements containing vague language terms (fast, quick, easy, simple, etc.)

analysis.manual: 6 out of 12 requirements contain ambiguous language
analysis.automated: 5 out of 12 requirements contain ambiguous language
analysis.hybrid: 1 out of 12 requirements contains ambiguous language
analysis.interpretation: Hybrid significantly improves clarity through selective human refinement

key_insights.test_coverage_strategy: Manual pipeline invests 2× test cases per requirement for thorough scenario coverage, while automated and hybrid use 1:1 mapping for lean, focused testing
key_insights.ambiguity_reduction: Hybrid pipeline reduces ambiguous language by 83% compared to automated (1 vs 5 requirements), achieving 0.0833 ambiguity ratio through selective wording refinement of P2, P3, P5
key_insights.review_evidence: Automated and hybrid pipelines evidence 60 reviews (grouped into 5 groups of 12 each); manual pipeline uses 48 reviews (4 groups of 12, 1 group of 0), reflecting manual creation constraints
key_insights.traceability_parity: All three pipelines achieve perfect traceability (ratio=1.0) and testability (rate=1.0), demonstrating that all requirements are source-attributed and test-covered regardless of pipeline strategy
key_insights.dataset_efficiency: Using 60 grouped reviews from 1,617 total achieves 3.7% dataset coverage with full traceability and test coverage, balancing evidence depth with practical specification scope

manual_strengths: Comprehensive test coverage with 24 fine-grained test scenarios (2 per requirement)
Direct human judgment for all artifacts, minimizing automation bias
Evidence-based creation from initial coding/grouping work
manual_limitations: Lower review evidence (60 → 48 reviews), reflecting resource constraints
Higher ambiguity ratio (0.5) suggesting potential clarity improvements
automated_strengths: Rapid generation of complete specifications from review groups
Consistent algorithmic application across all artifacts
Moderate ambiguity (0.4167) balanced with automation speed
automated_limitations: 1:1 test mapping may miss edge cases vs. manual's 2× coverage
No human refinement; inherits vagueness from review language
hybrid_strengths: Combines automated efficiency with selective human refinement
Dramatically improved clarity: 0.0833 ambiguity ratio (83% reduction vs. automated)
Balanced test coverage (1:1 mapping with improved acceptance criteria)
Evidence showing human judgment can refine generated specifications
comparison_narrative.hybrid_approach: Copy automated outputs, then selectively refine personas (P2, P3, P5) and requirements to match human quality expectations while preserving automation advantages
